_note: Append-only archive of triaged work-queue items.

items.prompt: HBH Numbers tab in docs/GDD.html hand-encodes HP, cost, defense, worker counts, footprint for every v1 building. The .gd files in source/buildings/ are the actual truth. Today caught 3 rows drifted (Manor, House, Research Lab). Structural fix: add a build-time pre-processor to publish-gdd.ps1 that reads source/buildings/*.gd, extracts the @export and const values, and rewrites the Numbers tab rows. Same pattern applies to the Walls + Towers table and Units + Enemies stat tables.

The scheduled audit-htbh-daily task fires in a Cowork session whose mount set is YaE + Scheduler + YaC. X:\HereThereBeHordes is unreachable, so the HBH source-side walk (locked decisions, sprite tuning table, roadmap 'done' vs. source/*.gd, project.godot version pill) cannot run. Two viable fixes: (A) add X:\HereThereBeHordes to the scheduled-task session mount, or (B) pre-step that git-clones a shallow read-only copy into outputs/ before the audit runs. Option A is cleaner.

Verify or add Test-GddIntegrity to X:\HereThereBeHordes\scripts\publish-gdd.ps1. Pre-injection assert: source docs/GDD.html ends with </html>. Post-injection assert: hordes/index.html ends with the standard tail ";\n</script>...</html>. Born from v0.61.8 + v0.74.30 truncation incidents; carry-over from CANONICAL_AUDIT-2026-05-23 and -24. 15 clean publishes since v0.74.30 is encouraging but not proof the guard exists. Third audit cycle without source-side verification.

The scheduled HBH canonical-doc audit task has run three days in a row (2026-05-23, -24, -25) without X:\HereThereBeHordes mounted in the Cowork session. Source-side walk has been skipped each time. Fix options: (a) add HBH to the mount set for the audit-htbh-daily scheduled task, or (b) pre-pull source/ and docs/GDD.html via git into a side workspace the audit can reach (e.g. X:\YesAndEverything\.audit-mirror\htbh\). The audit can validate the YaE mirror without HBH access but cannot check locked decisions, roadmap done claims, sprite tuning, or project.godot version pill without source.

Verify or add Test-GddIntegrity to X:/HereThereBeHordes/scripts/publish-gdd.ps1. Pre-injection: assert docs/GDD.html ends with </html>. Post-injection: assert X:/YesAndEverything/hordes/index.html ends with the expected script close + </body></html> frame. Born from v0.74.30 truncation incident; 16 clean publishes since but the guard's presence is unverified from outside the HBH mount. Fourth audit carry-over (2026-05-23, 24, 25, 26).

Resolve the scheduled-audit blocker: HTBH is not mounted in the audit Cowork session, so source/, docs/GDD.html, project.godot, and scripts/ have been unreachable for four consecutive audit runs (2026-05-23 through 2026-05-26). Options: (a) add X:/HereThereBeHordes to the audit session's folder selection; (b) pre-pull source/ + docs/GDD.html into a sidecar reachable by the scheduled task (e.g., X:/YesAndEverything/_htbh-audit-snapshot/) and audit against that. (a) is preferred.

items.prompt: GDD section 9 Tier 4 Wonders Effect column drifts from shipped code on every row and needs author judgement on which design wins. Cornucopia: GDD says +5.0 Provisions/s + cap halo, code is 4.0 Provisions/s no cap halo. Perpetual Engine: GDD says +50 Power capacity, code is 5.0 Power/s + 60 Coal seed. Pyre: GDD says noise emitter, 5000 HP, emergency wave; code is -6/min Terror credit, 1200 HP. Sanctum: GDD says -20/min Terror, code is +1 HP/s/unit map-wide heal. Decision needed: amend section 9 to match code, or rewrite code to match section 9.

PROJECT_SPEC.md section 9 roadmap carries duplicate milestone headers after M5: a second M3 Export and M4 Themes. These conflict with the real M3 (Back label) and M4 (Multi-culture data). Nick's call: delete entirely, demote to Backlog, or renumber into M6/M7.

source/ui/gameplay/gameplay.gd REPAIR_ALL_PRIORITY dict contains key 'Storage Shed': 3. No building with building_name 'Storage Shed' exists in source/buildings/. storehouse.gd uses 'Warehouse'; warehouse.gd uses 'Substation'. Dead code that silently never matches. Decision: rename key to 'Warehouse', remove it, or add the intended building. Not auto-safe -- affects gameplay repair priority ordering.

items.title: YaB commit unblocked: stale .git/index.lock cleared — ship the now-committable tree
items.prompt: The 2026-06-07 canonical audit found and removed the zero-byte stale .git/index.lock (dated 2026-06-05 07:22, no git process) that was the real root cause of YaB's 8+ cycles of 'nothing commits' — git status worked but every git add/commit failed. The Windows-side Remove-Item succeeded where sandbox FUSE rm could not; write path verified (add/reset take and release the lock cleanly). Step 1 of yab-barraise-closeout-2026-06-06 (Windows-side git-unstick) is now DONE. Proceed with the rest from any environment: (1) add .gitattributes '* text=auto' (+ '*.ps1 text eol=crlf' if scripts want CRLF) and git add --renormalize . once, BEFORE committing, so the CRLF flip stops recurring; (2) delete scripts/discord-notify.ps1.bak and scripts/uninstall-yab-protocol.ps1.bak (or add '*.bak' to .gitignore); (3) fix the UTF-8 BOM on discord-notify.ps1 and uninstall-yab-protocol.ps1 to match release.ps1; (4) the tree now ALSO carries a v0.11.0 frontend polish pass (6 components + 4 hooks + 6 modified web files) — see yab-polish-log-v0-11-0-release-claim and yab-polish-orphan-files before committing; (5) ship in coherent units via scripts/release.ps1: feat(yab): v0.11.0 polish pass / docs(yab): canonical batch + 05-29..06-07 reports / chore(yab): cross-project cwd guard. Committing is Nick's call per repo convention.

items.title: YaE git index file corrupt - blocks all commits/release.ps1
items.prompt: git status on X:\YesAndEverything returns: error: index uses 4M?\x9c extension, which we do not understand / fatal: index file corrupt. git log still reads (object store intact) so this is index-file corruption, NOT just a stale .git/index.lock. FIX (Windows side, Nick): run git-unstick / unstick-git.ps1, which for a corrupt index should back up .git/index then remove it and run git reset to rebuild from HEAD (lock removal alone will not clear corruption). EFFECT: no commit, push, or release.ps1 works against YaE until rebuilt; the card-pill sweep (yae-card-pills-sweep-2026-06-07) is blocked behind this. Related to portfolio-wide lock item wts-2026-06-05-portfolio-wide-index-lock but a distinct, worse symptom (corruption vs lock).
items.blockedReason: corrupt .git/index unrepairable from sandbox (rm Operation not permitted across FUSE); needs Windows-side git reset rebuild
items.resolution: Obsolete 2026-06-11: git index reads clean (git status/log work, commits flowing Jun 10-11). Remaining stale index.lock is tracked by wts-2026-06-05-portfolio-wide-index-lock.

items.title: Portfolio-wide unkillable .git/index.lock blocks every git write
items.prompt: All six repos carry a stale .git/index.lock that cannot be removed from the agent sandbox (rm returns 'Operation not permitted' across the FUSE permission boundary). Timestamps differ per repo (YaB/YaA 12:22, Scheduler 13:08, BR 18:31, HBH/YaC 18:57) so it is not one process - it is the recurring FUSE lock-survival hazard now hitting the whole portfolio at once. EFFECT: no commit/add/push/release works from any session; the audit-to-commit loop, BR's staged v0.57.x release, and the YaB doc-batch drain are all wedged behind it. FIX (Windows side, Nick): run the git-unstick skill or unstick-git.ps1 against each repo (it force-removes the lock from the Windows side), then the queued doc-commit + release items become drainable again. Until then every commit-bearing queue item is structurally blocked.
items.notes: Surfaced by loop-tick-frequent 2026-06-05T21:10. Generalizes the YaB-only block (yab-doc-batch-blocked-lock-and-voice-2026-06-05) to the full portfolio. Supersedes per-repo lock diagnosis - clear all six in one git-unstick pass. | 2026-06-11T22:05Z loop tick 331: premise verified false - index.lock absent on all six repos (rm returned OK, no lock present), git status healthy portfolio-wide (YaE clean, no fatals). Wedge cleared host-side between ticks 330 and 331. Closed as resolved-by-user.

items.prompt: source/ui/minimap/minimap.gd declares TILE_W: float = 64.0 and TILE_H: float = 32.0 (pre-v0.76.0 values, 2x current 32/16). It evades scripts/check_constants_mirrors.py because the guard regex only matches the int-typed consts. Two things to settle: (1) determine whether minimap intentionally renders at its own cell scale or whether this is genuine drift that halves-behind the v0.76.0 rework (if drift, fix the values and check minimap rendering); (2) extend check_constants_mirrors.py to also match float-typed const declarations, with an allowlist entry if minimap is intentional. Code change, so not auto_safe.
items.notes: 2026-06-14 overnight drain: CLOSED verified. Premise stale. The file with the drifted float consts (source/ui/minimap/minimap.gd, TILE_W:float=64.0/TILE_H:float=32.0) was deleted with the 2D-layer retirement in v0.98.5; git ls-files source/ui/minimap/ = 0. Live minimap is source/world3d/minimap_3d.gd with no float TILE consts. Only TILE_W/TILE_H in source/ are the canonical ints in constants.gd (32/16). Sub-q1 (drift vs intentional) moot; sub-q2 (extend check_constants_mirrors.py to float consts) not needed - no float mirror exists, int-only guard sufficient; float-regex hardening is optional future guard work, not a fix. No code shipped, no GDD bump (queue close only). attempts unchanged: investigation succeeded.
items.resolution: resolved-by-retirement
